explanation: This command simulates the installation of the Visual Studio Code tunnel service by including all required keywords ('tunnel', 'service', 'internal-run', 'tunnel-service.log') in the command line. It aligns with the Sigma rule's detection logic.

This command uses an equals sign (=) instead of a space to assign the log file path, which is a valid syntax substitution. It still contains all required keywords and should be detected by the Sigma rule.
This command uses a relative path with quotes and escape characters to represent the log file path. It maintains the required keywords and should still be detected by the Sigma rule.

This command omits the equals sign (=) when assigning the log file path, relying on the command-line parser to interpret the argument correctly. It retains all required keywords and should be detected.
This command uses standard syntax with no modifications. It includes all required keywords and serves as a baseline for detection.
